Zintlr [...] It sounds awesome, but once in I see nowhere how to get personality intel of a contact I've found. Ther is also tons of apparent features whose function are not very intuitive. In other words, I'm a bit lost. I believe you should seriously improve onboarding and UX. Your first menu item (FEED) is a coming soon page: for newcomers to your site that will be the most clicked menu item, so you are giving away a perception of uncompleteness. There's no help or knowledgebase, beside chat support where you request an email address I asked for help by chat and was told to install the Chrome extension for intel. I did and opened Linkedin, went to a profile, opened the extension and after 15 minutes it's still asking me to click on REFRESH (Processing your data. Please refresh.) After first refresh [...] Subscribe

results.title: Baserow Reviews (2026) - Product Hunt
results.content:
Baserow is far and away the best no-code solution for data management. But it is better than most custom solutions also. I am a system designer and prefer Baserow to coding a new data management landscape. My company builds extremely valuable data sets with Baserow in no time. This allows us to focus exclusively on obtaining great data and organizing it in the best way to reveal its true value. We easily export and continually update the extremely valuable data we can rest assured is always there, and always evolving in Baserow. We tried all the other options first. I personally tested each option because of how important data management is to my organization. We self-host, stay connected with the Baserow community, and completely trust the integrity and performance of this awesome system [...] I've been using Baserow for two years now, and it's been pretty awesome. What I especially love about it is how easy it is to self-host. Baserow provides a really well-built Docker image, which makes it a breeze to set up, update, and maintain. On the functional side, Baserow comes with a well-designed user interface that's both easy to understand and efficient to work with. The Application Builder is also great, it lets you build powerful applications on top of your databases. If you're into connecting different apps and making them work together, Baserow's API is very powerful. It turns Baserow into a great hub for all sorts of automations. All in all, Baserow is a rock-solid, well-rounded product with a fantastic set of features. Whether you're experienced building databases or just [...] 4.7(50 reviews)

Wow Samuel! Incredible to hear stuff like this. I really love that you highlighted avoiding bad stocks in addition to finding good stocks. As a user myself, I almost enjoy the features around avoiding bad stocks more than the features to find good stocks. We appreciate your continued support as a subscriber :)

Report

•1 review [...] One of the best stock analysis tool at your disposal. It's purposefully built for a amateur retail investor like me. Extremely easy to use, it comes with an additional educational feature which you can enable or disable depending upon your needs. If you compare with other stock and investment advice companies it's like a large one stop DIY shop. If you combine it with Daniel's YouTube videos you get a stock recommendation and analysis tool both rolled into one at one fourth the cost of professional agencies. As far as customer service is concerned, I can say it with conviction that these guys are definitely not here to make money, they are just on a mission to make everyone a great investor. Seven day free trial without pledging your credit card proves this beyond doubt. Any problem with [...] Report

•1 review

The app is really very useful and serves as ONE STOP SHOP for everything a long term investor needs to know when making an INFORMED decision. I really like all the graphs and visual aids as well as key data that are important in order to understand the fundamentals of the company. Well done Daniel, this app is fully aligned with your mission - to educate people and make them from good or not so good :) to great investors.

results.title: Slack Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Slack has truly transformed the way teams communicate and collaborate. It's more than just a messaging app — it brings structure, clarity, and efficiency to workplace conversations. The real-time messaging, file sharing, integration with tools like Google Drive, Trello, and GitHub, and the ability to create dedicated channels for projects or teams make it a powerful platform for productivity. I especially appreciate: Clean and intuitive UI: It’s easy to navigate and customize. Search functionality: Finding past messages or shared documents is a breeze. Threaded conversations: Helps keep discussions organized and on-topic. Notifications control: You can fine-tune what alerts you get, which is a blessing. That said, it can sometimes feel a bit overwhelming in very active workspaces, [...] real-time messaging(58)

•5 reviews

#### What's great

I've been using Slack for team communication, and it's made collaboration much easier. Channels keep conversations organized, and the integrations with tools like Notion and Google Drive save a lot of time.

#### What needs improvement [...] Report

 65 views

•4 reviews

We use Slack because it's kind of the middle ground between Google Chat and Microsoft Teams, which both are not that great. Slack is a good messenger tool. If you use it just as a chat, trying to integrate anything else into it, and/or trying to juggle multiple workspaces, or get people onboarded who have never used it before is a bit challenging. I think Slack was better before they tried to add all of the features that all of the other tools most companies already use. It feels less polished, kind of clunky, and difficult to navigate. Conversation threads, lose messages and get buried if you get too many messages in a day. We use it because there's nothing else really. Would love to see a Slack Lite with just messaging and no other features.

results.title: QA.tech Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Patrick Lef
QA.tech

QA.tech

Thanks for checking us out and thanks for the feedback! We will make sure to make it clearer that no sensitive users or passwords should be used as config for your test cases.  
  
The thing is that it's really hard to keep the password safe as there's network logs, console logs, browser states, and LLM calls involved so we have chosen to store and show as plain text to be transparent about that no sensitive configs should be added.

AI agents (transformer LLMs) use plain text indeed, so it makes sense you've seen your credential exposed.   
We recommend our users create dedicated accounts for AI agents or hide their dev/staging under SSH tunnels:   
  
But ideally you're right, we should make a way for our AI agent to use credentials in a more secure way.
